summary:
Claim: The line connecting the distal to the lingual aspect is the longest.
Evidence: 
- Maxillary molar roots project into a triangular pattern where the distal-to-lingual line varies more widely due to individual tooth morphology.
- Enamel ridges and developmental factors contribute to this variance.
Answer: C
You've accurately captured the essence of why the distal-to-lingual line is the longest based on common maxillary molar anatomy. Well done!
